blocks.content: He showed up at eleven twenty-eight. Mrs. Jones handed him a paper plate and put him to work immediately.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: You can flip the burgers.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yes ma'am.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: He walked in thirty seconds ago.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: And now he has a job. Welcome back to Georgia, Kit.
They ate on the back porch — burgers and potato salad and sweet tea. Mrs. Jones talked about the neighborhood.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: You two going to Eli's thing tonight.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yes ma'am.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: That boy called here in March. Asked me if I thought the field had enough clearance for aerial fireworks.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: He called you.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: He was very thorough about it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: He had a spreadsheet.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: I know. He sent it to me.
Kit looked at Daisy. She was already pressing her lips together to keep from laughing.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Of course he did.
By five o'clock the light had gone gold and long across the yard and Mrs. Jones was doing dishes and Kit and Daisy were on the back steps saying nothing in particular.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We should head over around six fifteen.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Eli said six.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Eli will be there at five-thirty setting things up. We don't need to be first.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Six fifteen.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Six fifteen.
She leaned back on her palms. The sky above the yard was starting to go pink at the edges.
